meta.description: Superblock field mapping for xfs: kernel (6.12) → mkfs.xfs (xfsprogs 6.12) → ConfD mkfs_xfs_constraints
meta.struct_mkfs: struct mkfs_params + struct sb_feat_args (mkfs in-memory config)

superblock_field_layout.doc: Superblock CRC (V5 only, not copied to in-memory)

disk_to_mem_mappings.detail: to->sb_gquotino = be64_to_cpu(from->sb_gquotino); 0→NULLFSINO; if PQUOTA old: copy to pquotino
to->sb_pquotino = be64_to_cpu(from->sb_pquotino); may get gquotino value for old format
if V5+META_UUID: uuid_copy(from sb_meta_uuid); else: uuid_copy(from sb_uuid)
to->sb_crc = 0 (on-disk CRC not copied to memory)

confd_to_superblock.note: -b size: stored be32. blocklog = log2(blocksize) stored separately
-l sectsize: log device sector size; stored be16
-n size: stored as log2 relative to fs block: sb_dirblklog = log2(dirblocksize) - sb_blocklog
single u8; log2 of dir block size in fs blocks
-i size: stored be16; must be power of 2, 256..2048
-i perblock: inodes per block = blocksize / inodesize; stored be16
derived: rtblocks/rextsize; not stored in superblock
confd_to_superblock.formula: rtextblocks := rtblocks / rextsize (not stored in superblock)

confd_to_superblock.note: -d sunit: data stripe unit in fsblocks; stored be32
-d swidth: data stripe width in fsblocks; stored be32
-l sunit: log stripe unit in blocks; stored be32
-d agsize: AG size in fsblocks; stored be32
-i maxpct: max % of fs for inodes; single u8
-l internal: log inside fs (1) or external (0); affects logstart choice
-l dev: starting block of internal log; stored be64
-l agnum: used to compute logstart; not stored directly
confd_to_superblock.formula: logagno: used to compute logstart; not stored directly

confd_to_superblock.note: internal: composite bitmask set on versionnum+features2
-i align: inode chunk alignment in fsblocks; stored be32

confd_to_superblock.formula: sb_features2 or sb_features_incompat := sb_features2 or sb_features_incompat OR 0x0010 or 0x0080
sb_features2(V4) or sb_features_incompat(V5) := sb_features2(V4) or sb_features_incompat(V5) OR 0x0200 or 0x0001

confd_to_superblock.note: -i sparse=1: sets XFS_SB_FEAT_INCOMPAT_SPINODES; also sets sb_spino_align
-d noalign: disables AG data alignment; runtime only
-r noalign: disables RT extent alignment; runtime only

usage_notes.general_mechanism: mkfs.xfs writes parameters to an on-disk xfs_dsb (big-endian format, 512 bytes). The kernel converts to host-endian xfs_sb via __xfs_sb_from_disk(). Feature bits are spread across 4 fields: sb_versionnum, sb_features2, sb_features_ro_compat, sb_features_incompat.
usage_notes.big_endian: XFS is the ONLY filesystem using big-endian on-disk format. All multi-byte fields require beXX_to_cpu() conversion. Single-byte fields (blocklog, sectlog, inodelog, etc.) have no endian conversion.
usage_notes.v4_v5_superblock: V4 superblock uses versionnum + features2 fields for feature bits. V5 superblock (CRC enabled) uses versionnum + features2 + features_compat/ro_compat/incompat. Some bits migrate between fields: dirftype is in features2(V4) or incompat(V5).
usage_notes.dirblocksize_calculation: dirblocksize is stored as sb_dirblklog = log2(dirblocksize/fs_blocksize). NOT an absolute log2. e.g. dirblocksize=4096, blocksize=4096, sb_dirblklog=0.
usage_notes.quota_remap: In non-V5 superblocks, quota inode values of 0 are converted to NULLFSINO. OQUOTA_ENFD/CHKD flags are remapped to actual PQUOTA or GQUOTA depending on which quota type is active.
usage_notes.feature_count: Feature bits are spread across 4 different bitmask fields with different kernel access functions. The V5 superblock format consolidates newer features into the *_compat series.
